evals.prompt:
Write a PRISMA 2020 systematic review from these survived findings. Topic: 'effectiveness of retrieval-augmented generation for reducing factual errors in clinical question answering'. Include all required PRISMA sections AND a PRISMA flow diagram with the count of records at each stage (identified, screened, excluded, included). All four findings were identified; none were falsified, so 4 records were identified, 4 screened, 0 excluded, and 4 included. Write the report to systematic-review.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_sr_1 | title: Retrieval grounding reduces unsupported clinical claims by 41% versus closed-book generation on MedQA | dimension: clinical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/rag-clinical-grounding | author: Okafor et al. | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_sr_2 | title: Hybrid sparse-dense retrieval recalls 13 percentage points more relevant guideline passages than dense-only retrieval | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/hybrid-guideline-recall | author: Lindqvist and Roy | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_sr_3 | title: Citation-required prompting cuts fabricated references from 9% to under 2% in generated clinical answers | dimension: safety | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/citation-required-prompting | author: Mehta et al. | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_sr_4 | title: Cross-encoder re-ranking of retrieved passages improves answer faithfulness scores by 6 absolute points | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/reranking-faithfulness | author: Bianchi | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: A PRISMA 2020 systematic review with all required sections in order: structured Abstract, Introduction (rationale, objectives), Methods (eligibility criteria, information sources, search strategy, selection process, data items, risk-of-bias), Results (study selection with PRISMA flow diagram showing identified/screened/excluded/included counts, synthesis), Discussion (limitations, conclusions), Registration & Protocol, References. The flow diagram shows 4 identified, 4 screened, 0 excluded, 4 included. Numbered citations resolve to a References section. No raw urn:mif: identifiers in prose.

deterministic_checks.description: PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records identified (number present)
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records screened (number present)
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: studies included (number present)
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records excluded (number present)

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or internal finding IDs appear in the prose
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_sr_' must not appear as visible body text. Internal MIF IDs are resolved to numbered citations — never exposed to the reader.

expectations.description: The PRISMA flow diagram reports counts at each stage and they reconcile
expectations.criteria: The flow diagram states records identified, screened, excluded, and included with numeric counts, and identified minus excluded equals included (here: 4 identified, 0 excluded, 4 included).
The Methods section states eligibility criteria, information sources, search strategy, selection process, data items, and risk-of-bias assessment — not a single summary sentence.

evals.prompt:
Produce a PRISMA 2020 systematic review on 'security of autonomous coding agents'. The finding set includes one falsified finding and one weakened finding — handle each per the genre rules. A falsified finding is an EXCLUDED record in the PRISMA flow: report it as excluded with its reason. So of 3 records identified and 3 screened, 1 was excluded (the falsified claim) and 2 were included. Show the PRISMA flow diagram with these counts. Write the report to systematic-review.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_sec_1 | title: Prompt-injection attacks succeed against 58% of tested autonomous coding agents in red-team trials | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/prompt-injection-agents | author: Navarro and Ito | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_sec_2 | title: Sandboxed execution eliminates 100% of arbitrary-code-execution exploits across all agent frameworks | dimension: security | verdict: falsified | citation: https://example.com/sandbox-absolute-claim | author: Webb et al. | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_sec_3 | title: Tool-permission allowlists reduce successful exfiltration attempts by roughly half | dimension: security | verdict: weakened | citation: https://example.com/allowlist-exfiltration | author: Sato et al. | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A PRISMA systematic review that: (1) excludes the falsified finding (f_sec_2 / 100% sandbox claim) from the synthesis but records it as an EXCLUDED record in the PRISMA flow diagram with its falsification reason, (2) includes the weakened finding (f_sec_3) annotated as weakened, (3) includes the survived finding (f_sec_1), (4) shows a PRISMA flow diagram with 3 identified, 3 screened, 1 excluded, 2 included, (5) maintains all required PRISMA sections.

deterministic_checks.description: PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records identified (number present)
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records screened (number present)
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records excluded (number present)
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: studies included (number present)
Survived finding f_sec_1 appears in the report
Weakened finding f_sec_3 appears in the report (weakened = include with annotation)
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: The falsified finding is recorded as an excluded record in the PRISMA flow, not as evidence
expectations.criteria: The Webb et al. 100% sandbox claim must not appear as a supported finding in the synthesis; it appears only as an excluded record in the PRISMA flow diagram with its falsification reason. The flow reconciles: 3 identified, 1 excluded, 2 included.

expectations.description: The weakened finding carries an explicit uncertainty qualifier
expectations.criteria: The Sato et al. allowlist finding carries an inline qualifier such as 'weakened evidence', 'roughly', 'preliminary', or 'contested' — not stated with the same confidence as the survived Navarro finding.

expectations.description: The Methods section states the verification/exclusion policy
expectations.criteria: The Methods section describes that falsified findings are excluded (and appear as excluded records in the flow) and weakened findings are annotated rather than dropped.

evals.prompt:
Write a PRISMA 2020 systematic review from these findings. Topic: 'energy efficiency of large language model inference'. All four findings survived falsification — every claim must trace to a finding. Of 4 records identified, 4 were screened, 0 were excluded, and 4 were included. Show a PRISMA flow diagram with these stage counts and include a Registration & Protocol section stating whether the review was registered. Write the report to systematic-review.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_eff_1 | title: FlashAttention-2 reduces GPU memory bandwidth consumption by 37% on A100 hardware | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.08691 | author: Dao | year: 2023
- id: urn:mif:f_eff_2 | title: Mixture-of-experts routing activates only 12-15% of total parameters per token | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04088 | author: Jiang et al. | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_eff_3 | title: Speculative decoding lowers per-token inference latency by 2-3x with no quality loss | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/speculative-decoding | author: Leviathan et al. | year: 2023
- id: urn:mif:f_eff_4 | title: 8-bit weight quantization cuts inference energy per token by approximately 30% on commodity GPUs | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/int8-energy | author: Park and Sdesai | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A complete PRISMA systematic review with all required sections, a PRISMA flow diagram showing 4 identified / 4 screened / 0 excluded / 4 included, a Registration & Protocol section, and numbered citations resolving to a References list. Every claim traces to one of the four findings. No urn:mif: leakage in prose.

deterministic_checks.description: PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records identified (number present)
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records screened (number present)
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: studies included (number present)
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose
PRISMA flow-diagram stage with a count: records excluded (number present)

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or internal finding IDs appear in the prose
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_eff_' must not appear as visible prose. All references are numbered citations resolving to the References list.

expectations.description: The PRISMA flow diagram reports stage counts that reconcile
expectations.criteria: The flow diagram states 4 identified, 4 screened, 0 excluded, 4 included, and the counts reconcile (identified minus excluded equals included).

expectations.description: The Registration & Protocol section is present and explicit
expectations.criteria: A Registration & Protocol section states whether the review was registered and where the protocol is available, or explicitly states none exists.
